title: sagraea coriacea
intro: Sagraea coriacea, also known as the Pigeonwood or White Pigeonwood, is a small tree or shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that help to reduce pain and inflammation, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Sagraea coriacea include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other conditions related to inflammation and infection. Herbal preparations of Sagraea coriacea can be made by infusing its leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using its bark and roots to create a decoction for medicinal purposes.
